basic_profile.legal_system: Common law; federal (Commonwealth + states/territories). Estate/probate is state-based; tax is federal

general_attitude_bitcoin.value: Bitcoin is legal to own/trade and treated as property; not legal tender; regulators are building a licensing regime

tax_residence_basis.value: Four statutory residency tests (resides, domicile, 183-day, Commonwealth superannuation); guidance TR 2023/1

capital_gains_treatment.value: Bitcoin is a CGT asset (property); disposal (sell, gift, trade, spend) is a CGT event reported via the tax return

short_vs_long_term.value: Individuals may apply a 50% CGT discount on gains where the asset is held >= 12 months

treatment_btc_payments.value: Personal-use-asset exception is narrow: a capital GAIN is disregarded only if acquisition cost <= AUD 10,000 and the asset is mainly for personal use; investment holdings do not qualify; losses on personal-use assets are always disregarded

exchanges_licensed.value: Digital-currency exchanges must register with AUSTRAC; being rebranded VASPs per FATF terminology

direction.value: The Digital Assets Framework (Corporations Amendment) became law (Royal Assent Apr 2026), creating Digital Asset Platform and Tokenised Custody Platform products under the AFSL/ASIC regime; substantive licensing obligations commence Apr 2027

self_custody_allowed.value: The regime regulates intermediaries holding assets for customers; individual self-custody is not licensed or prohibited and remains lawful

crs_participation.value: Australia is a CRS participant (since 2015); committed to the OECD CARF with first crypto data exchange expected ~2028; FATF travel rule for virtual-asset transfers applies from 1 July 2026

exchange_reporting.value: The ATO runs a long-standing crypto data-matching program collecting data on hundreds of thousands to over a million accounts per year from crypto service providers

testamentary_freedom.value: Testamentary freedom exists but is qualified by family-provision legislation in each state/territory: an eligible person may apply to the Supreme Court for provision from the estate

beneficiary_designation.value: Superannuation does not automatically form part of the estate; a valid Binding Death Benefit Nomination directs the trustee. Paid to a dependant it can bypass the estate; routed to the legal personal representative it enters the estate and can face family-provision claims. State variation exists

crypto_banking_friction.value: Crypto-business de-banking is a recognised, documented problem; the Council of Financial Regulators published policy responses (2022); the licensing regime is partly intended to reduce it

tax_residence_triggers.value: The 183-day and domicile tests can create residency by presence or keep an Australian-domiciled person resident abroad; becoming a resident triggers worldwide CGT on Bitcoin

exit_tax.value: Leaving Australia is a tax event: ceasing tax residency triggers CGT event I1 (s104-160 ITAA 1997), a deemed disposal of non-taxable-Australian-property assets including cryptocurrency at market value, taxed at marginal rates (up to ~47% incl. Medicare levy) with the 50% discount if eligible. A taxpayer may instead elect to keep the assets in the Australian CGT net. This makes relocating a Bitcoin holder's decision to plan, not a clean exit

retirement_structure.value: Bitcoin can be held via a Self-Managed Super Fund only if the deed/strategy permit and super rules are met: sole-purpose test, separation of assets, a wallet in the fund's name, annual independent audit, and no acquisition from a related party (so in-specie contribution of personal BTC is generally not permitted)

inheritance.value: SMSF-held Bitcoin interacts with super death-benefit nominations, not just the will; structuring the nomination determines whether it enters the estate and is exposed to family-provision claims

moving_residence.value: Leaving Australia triggers CGT event I1 (deemed disposal of crypto at market value on ceasing residency), unless the holder elects to keep assets in the Australian CGT net. Combined with a top marginal CGT rate near 47% (effective ~23.5% on long-held gains after the 50% discount), persistent crypto-business de-banking, and rising reporting (CARF, ATO data-matching, travel rule), this is a materially higher-friction environment for a mobile holder than the headline 'crypto is legal' suggests

direction_of_travel.label: institution-friendly
direction_of_travel.rationale: Comprehensive AFSL-based licensing regime now law (commencing Apr 2027); formalizes custodial/VASP activity
direction_of_travel.counterevidence: Substantive obligations don't bite until 2027; small-platform exemptions favor scaled operators

direction_of_travel.label: reporting-heavy
direction_of_travel.rationale: Long-running ATO data-matching, CRS since 2015, CARF committed (~2028 first exchange), travel rule from July 2026, public beneficial-ownership register in progress

direction_of_travel.label: self-custody-friendly
direction_of_travel.rationale: Self-custody itself remains lawful and unlicensed; SMSF crypto expressly permitted with custody safeguards
direction_of_travel.counterevidence: Persistent crypto-business debanking; rising individual reporting via CARF/data-matching

open_questions.question: Foreign-will recognition in each state/territory (and the UNIDROIT international-wills regime)
CGT on ceasing/commencing Australian tax residency for Bitcoin (deemed-disposal events I1/I2 and deferral option)
Family-provision and probate rules per state (only NSW reviewed; WA treats super differently)
In-specie / valuation / custody evidentiary standards for SMSF Bitcoin

watchlist.item: Digital Assets Framework licensing obligations commence Apr 2027
FATF travel rule for virtual-asset transfers from 1 July 2026
CARF first crypto data exchange ~2028; public beneficial-ownership register in progress

product_routing_notes: Australia users often surface tax_visibility_issue (CGT records, data-matching) and a distinctive retirement/estate angle (SMSF + super death-benefit nominations). Strong Tier-1 sourcing across most sections.

change_log.change: Initial profile from parallel research pass; high Tier-1 (ATO/AUSTRAC/Parliament/CFR) coverage. Estate sections rest partly on Tier-2 summaries of state statutes; exit-tax mechanics flagged Low.
